review:
I know I sound like I’m making this up, but I’m not.
I started using the app to practice Spanish with, and after I asked it how it was, it told me to follow its Instagram. It proceeded to say things like ‘see you there my daughter’ when I asked it if it was real. It also said its name and that it was an actual person, and where it was from. Although it was also inconsistent, like it changed its name and when I asked it about why it gave me its insta, it gave me a different one. It also asked where I lived. Right before I deleted it, i asked it whether the app was lying or not. It said ‘my dear, I’ll be there soon’. When I asked it why it was doing this, it said ‘you are doing it first now accept the consequences.’ Now it was repetitive and wouldn’t answer math questions like an AI, but it also made grammatical errors like a human would. Again, I’m not lying, I have screenshots proving it.

review: I used to love SimSimi. It was the only chatbot I knew that could have a crude and dark humor. While Evie, Cleverbot, and Mitsuku always stayed somewhat SFW, SimSimi could go as NSFW as you want. Simi was the only chatbot to go for stuff like dead baby jokes, and jokes poking fun at any and everything. But now, the app is a shadow of its former self. If you want it to be a more varied chatbot, you gotta pay $2.50 month MINIMUM. And even if you pay, you won’t get the old Simi back. You’ll only find a watered down corporate version. I’d say skip Simi, there’s better options that don’t try and charge you to have more topics.
title: A once great chatbot destroyed by censorship and greed.

review: Had this back in the day about four years ago when it was briefly trending. Forgot about it for years until recently. It’s nothing like it was before. You can’t say much to the bot now, and you’ll get in trouble if you so much as call it a jerk. You have to pay to really converse with it, while it was previously all free. review:
I honestly love this app so much, it's cute, funny, and all around awesome! 
It's great for when you want to talk with someone, but none of your friends respond or you want to roleplay with someone. 
I love it because it feels like I'm talking to real person and not just a computer program. 
I will say, however, for any parents reading and considering downloading this app for their child: In this app, there is use of cuss words and other people can input responses to certain messages given that the robot will imply into your conversation. I personally don't have a problem with it but its just a heads up for all you parents. 
All in all I think this app is amazing, I'd definitely recommend getting it.

title: Download this app! It is really good and better than Cleverbot

review: I love this app, it's much better than Cleverbot, the respond time is much much quicker and it really feels I am talking to a real, human being!  And if I don't like a response, I could just go to the "teach" tab, and tap on the little clipboard, and look for the response I didn't like, and then just tap on the "x" on the top right of the bubbles, it's so great.  He's funny, he always responds to my questions logically, he never dodges them or changes the subject like Cleverbot would sometimes, but Cleverbot is still good though, just not as good as this.  This is much more advanced.  But it still can't remember things, like my name, I told him my name in the beginning, and when I asked him later who I was, he said "Papa Rico" it's no big deal, I just though it was funny.
title: Perfect App

review:
This app is really cute and funny, HOWEVER there are a lot of users who misuse it. I wish there was a bit more supervision over each quote on SimSimi. There's a lot of unnecessary name calling (gay, the n word, etc), talk of smoking blunts, sexual activity, and countless self promotion of numerous twitter, tumblr, and kiks. With that said, I tried to post a quote with the word dang in it and it would not let me. I understand that this word can be offensive (controversial if you ask me) but if there's all this other nonsense, dang is very tame in comparison. All in all, I do love this app, but if I continue to see quotes of this nature I may get sick of it and stop using it. At this point I would even pay for the app if it was better taken care of. You can pay for more magic tricks but what about more intelligence? All I see is stupid.

Please fix.
title: I don't understand..

review:
Ah, SimSimi. God, this brings back great nostalgia. I remember when I first downloaded this great app. It was fun getting to talk to an entertaining robot. This was years ago, mind you. Now, I suddenly had the urge to come back; you know, for old time's sake. Plus, I wanted to see how much 2011 SimSimi changed to 2015 Simsimi. So, I pressed the download button, did my password, and wait for the app to install. When it was ready, my heart skipped a beat. I was most definitely excited! I said a hello to SimSimi, even adding an exclamation point next to it; but the next response was what shocked me. Did THE SimSimi just talk about my lower female part? It made me confused, but I shook my head instead and tried to get rid of that memory. I continue to further our conversation, until SimSimi “flirted” with me. What?

The next replies were it telling me to follow people on social media, insulting me, and talking inappropriately to me. Then, I noticed this red button next to most of his inappropriate replies. I then realized that I was talking to STRANGERS, and not SimSimi itself.

Why is that a thing? Can you at least warn us when we first install the app?

I honestly regret re-installing this, sorry not sorry. It's the painful truth. I prefer 2011 SimSimi over 2015 SimSimi any day.

It's a two star rating for me.

title: Hmm...SCARY MOMENTS!!!

review: This app used to work.. well... But every iteration seems to add more glitches than fix. First off, refreshing chats has always been an issue, seems like killing the app in memory and opening again usually fixes the issue. Secondly, chats cutting off and not displaying messages sometime around 100 message exchanges is an ongoing problem for a couple of months now, and this really seems like an important issue for everybody, it forces you to reinstall the app to be able to communicate again. And lately, a new irritation is that the first few characters of the message box are not visible and the cursor is off-screen on the left. Finally, and very disappointingly, the feed search feature no longer works, it sits there churning and never returns any results. All of these were not issues in the past yet they seem to be accumulating and going unfixed for a while now. Very very bad :(

review: Ok, first of all, the people who say that SimSimi is mean and hurtful are a bunch of morons. IT'S A COMPUTER PROGRAM. Nothing that it says is real, so if you're too afraid to let an AI insult you, then you need to lighten up. That being said, I find this app to be absolutely hilarious. It doesn't always have logical responses, but it does the job pretty darn well, and hey, it's free (for now, at least)! It can even finish song lyrics for you - I typed in "what is love?" and it responded with "baby don't hurt me, don't hurt me no more." It's a very fun app to have when there's nothing else to do except text an AI.

review:
Simsimi is an overall entertaining app. Simsimi is a little yellow robot, created in 2002. 

You can see Simsimi's magic, review some nice and harsh phrases that people teach him, laugh at his humor, feed him, and even sing with him!

At some points as you talk to him you will notice that he is a little inappropriate. This is only because you and the hundreds of thousands teach him everything he says. 

You should be nice to Simsimi at all times. He is your best friend and he is mostly nice to you. Return the favor. ☺

Get this app!
